pain_point_label: Reactive Crisis Communications — No Proactive Narrative

matches.match_rationale: The core deficit here is absence of proactive content: no op-eds, no student success stories, no thought leadership, no press releases. Text generation directly produces these artifacts on demand — principals, communications staff, or executives can trigger drafts from structured prompts and publish with light editing. Multi-site operators often lack dedicated PR writers, so AI-generated drafts are a force multiplier rather than a replacement.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI content pipeline that takes a trigger (student award, accreditation milestone, innovation launch) and auto-drafts a press release, social media post, and op-ed outline for review and sign-off — reducing time-to-publish from weeks to hours.

matches.match_rationale: Proactive PR requires a repeating workflow: monitor internal events → identify story-worthy moments → draft content → route for approval → distribute to media contacts. This is a multi-step process that currently falls through the cracks because no one owns it. A task automation agent can watch internal systems (student records events, award databases, calendar) and trigger the full drafting-and-routing workflow autonomously, closing the execution gap that keeps communications reactive.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated narrative engine that polls school event feeds weekly, scores items for media value, drafts a short-form story for flagged events, and emails a draft to the communications lead for one-click approval and distribution.

matches.match_rationale: A key reason institutions are reactive is that they don't monitor the media landscape: they don't track competitor narratives, don't know when critics are building coverage momentum, and miss windows to insert their own story. A research agent that continuously monitors news sources, social media, and regulatory bulletins for mentions of the institution, its sector, and competitors gives the communications team early warning and surfaced angles for proactive positioning.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A daily media intelligence brief auto-generated by an agent that scans news feeds, competitor press, and relevant regulatory or policy news — delivered to the communications lead each morning with flagged risks and recommended response angles.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators accumulate large volumes of internal material — annual reports, student outcome data, accreditation submissions, staff innovation projects — that contain compelling narrative material but never gets surfaced externally. Summarization enables communications staff to rapidly distill these dense internal documents into story briefs and talking points, making proactive outreach feasible without dedicated PR researchers.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document-to-story-brief tool where communications staff upload any internal report or data package and receive a 200-word narrative summary and three headline angles ready for media pitching.
